title: The Kato Equation: A Zero-Parameter Exponent Selection Rule for Urban Scaling
description: Working paper on a zero-parameter relay-depth ladder for urban scaling exponents. Tested against the Bettencourt et al. (PNAS 2007) 22-indicator panel: integer relay-depth predictions match 17/22 reported confidence intervals; network-anchored composition lifts coverage to 21/22; a label-free K=2 lower-valley mixture (H in {1,2}) for physical d=3 cities recovers all 22/22, with MAE = 0.031 and RMSE = 0.045. Patents land on H=2; GDP and wages occupy an H=4-5 corridor; infrastructure follows the negative branch. Paper 4 of the Kato Equation Working Paper Cluster (Papers 0-10 plus Manifesto 1/2 forthcoming). Release v1.1 (manuscript V128/V129) polish wave: Manifesto 1 / Manifesto 2 reframe, abstract correction to K=2 lower-valley H in {1,2}, SI cross-time beta table column-collision fix, data and code availability section forward-looking (concept DOI primary). Release v1.2 launch-ready. Closing Bettencourt's canonical 22-indicator urban panel with a zero-parameter relay-depth ladder. Building on the seminal Bettencourt et al. (PNAS 2007) framework, this manuscript proposes a zero-parameter Kato relay-depth ladder beta_pm(H) = 1 pm 1/[H ln(2H+1)] that closes all 22 indicators at the 95% CI: 17/22 at strict integer H, 21/22 with continuous H_eff, 22/22 under a label-free K=2 lower-valley mixture protocol (MAE 0.031, RMSE 0.045). Headline manuscript paired with a twelve-document cluster preview (Paper "-1" Manifesto 1 + Paper 0 Manifesto 2 + Papers 1-10) and one-click arXiv endorsement URLs for first-time submitter (physics.soc-ph + cs.AI). SI Appendix S-PanelOrigins: per-indicator panel with three coverage tiers (17 strict integer + 4 continuous H_eff + 1 K=2 cluster mixture); reading guide formalises three non-H-to-infinity paths to beta=1 (cross-branch sigma_gc=0 / continuous H_eff Jensen convexity / K=2 cluster integer mixture). SI Appendix S-CrossTimeBeta: 15-row cross-time beta reconstruction (hunter-gatherer H=1 through post-human substrate H=8) with canonical beta_pred values 1.311/1.171/1.114 for H=2/3/4. Bettencourt foundational reference acknowledged with framework-specific reframe for integer-ladder testing. All hyperlinks clickable; bibliography DOIs auto-clickable via natbib. Manuscript prepared through 162 iterations of careful manual revision and three rounds of independent peer-style audit before release.

creators.name: Kato, Shota
creators.affiliation: AI and Future Co., Ltd., Tokyo, Japan
